рҹҳөвҖҚрҹ’« hypnoR

Hypnogram handling, plotting, and sleep architecture metrics for R вҖ” the staging-agnostic layer of the Circadia Lab ecosystem.

рҹ“– What is hypnoR?

hypnoR is the hypnogram layer of the Circadia Lab R ecosystem. It sits between raw signal sources and the integrated participant database, providing a common interface for sleep staging regardless of where the staging came from.

It accepts two staging resolutions:

  • Full AASM (5-state: W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) вҖ” supplied by mrpheus
  • Coarse (3-state: W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) вҖ” supplied by zeitR

All metric functions are resolution-agnostic: they compute every metric that is possible given the available stages and return NA with an informative message for metrics that require full AASM staging.

Downstream, hypnoR feeds into syncR::sync() as part of the unified participant-indexed database.

вңЁ Features

  • Flexible ingestion вҖ” read_hypnogram() reads EDF annotations, CSV, YASA output, Compumedics Profusion, and Nox Medical formats.

  • Architecture metrics вҖ” compute_sleep_architecture() returns TST, SE, SOL, WASO, REM latency, SWS latency, and stage percentages; all metrics degrade gracefully for coarse hypnograms.

  • Cycle segmentation вҖ” compute_cycles() detects NREM/REM cycles via Feinberg & Floyd (1979) or AASM rules.

  • Transition analysis вҖ” compute_transitions() builds a stage-to-stage transition probability matrix and computes a fragmentation index.

  • Publication-ready plots вҖ” plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), and plot_transition_matrix() all use theme_circadia() from the circadia shared visual identity package.

рҹ—ӮпёҸ Project Structure 

hypnoR/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ R/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hypnoR-package.R       # package-level docs
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ read_hypnogram.R       # ingestion
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ architecture.R         # compute_sleep_architecture()
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cycles.R               # compute_cycles()
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ transitions.R          # compute_transitions()
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ plot.R                 # plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(),
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                          #   plot_transition_matrix()
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ utils.R                # internal helpers
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tests/testthat/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ vignettes/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ getting-started.Rmd
в”ңв”Җв”Җ man/figures/               # logo, favicon, card
в”ңв”Җв”Җ .github/workflows/         # R CMD CHECK + pkgdown CI
в”ңв”Җв”Җ _pkgdown.yml
в””в”Җв”Җ DESCRIPTION

рҹҡҖ Getting Started

Prerequisites: R вүҘ 4.1, remotes.

remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/hypnoR")

Basic workflow:

library(hypnoR)

hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv")
arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp)
cyc  <- compute_cycles(hyp)           # full AASM only
trans <- compute_transitions(hyp)

plot_hypnogram(hyp)
plot_architecture(arch)
plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix)

рҹ“Ұ Dependencies

Package Role
cli User-facing messages and errors
dplyr Tabular data manipulation
lubridate Timestamp handling
rlang Tidy eval and error helpers
tibble Tidy output objects
tidyr Reshaping for transition matrices
ggplot2 (Suggests) Plotting
circadia (Suggests) Shared colour palette and theme_circadia()

рҹ‘Ҙ Authors

Role Name
Author, maintainer Lucas FranГ§a
Author Mario Leocadio-Miguel

Circadia Lab, Northumbria University.

рҹӨқ Related Tools

  • рҹ“Ұ zeitR вҖ” wrist actigraphy analysis and circadian metrics; upstream source of coarse hypnograms
  • рҹ“Ұ mrpheus вҖ” PSG signal analysis; upstream source of full AASM hypnograms
  • рҹ“Ұ syncR вҖ” integrates zeitR, slumbR, tallieR, and hypnoR into a unified participant-indexed database
  • рҹ“Ұ circadia вҖ” shared visual identity: palettes, themes, and scales
  • рҹ”¬ circadia-bio вҖ” the Circadia Lab GitHub organisation
